关于.text:
Skip to main content
About the Awards
The SBID International Interior Design Awards

The SBID International Design Awards has firmly established itself as one of the most prestigious interior design Awards in the global design calendar. 

This Award for design standards attracts entries from across the world, making it truly an honour and a sign of industry recognition that is second to none for the deserving winners.

The awards recognise, reward and celebrate design excellence across a broad range of categories spanning Interior Design, Fit-Out and Product. Winning a SBID International Design Award is a high achievement – they are evaluated by leading industry experts for their technical content and aesthetic creativity while the design-conscious public also have a say in voting for the most inspiring projects.

Rated GOLD Standard Awards 

The SBID International Design Awards became the first interior design award scheme to be accredited with the GOLD Standard Awards Trust Mark by The Independent Awards Standards Council, who commended the programme as “exemplar in how awards should be operated”.

Find out more
Awards Categories
Interior Design

Spanning residential and commercial sectors, Interior Design categories invite entries from a spectrum of projects; from restaurants, retail space and healthcare facilities, to offices, show homes and luxury apartments.

View the categories
Product Design

With a focus on the design, innovation and functionality of products for the interior design industry, the Product Design categories span a range of specialist sectors, judged by a panel of esteemed interior designers.

View the categories
Interior Fit-Out

The Interior Fit-Out categories are for interior contractors fulfilling the fit-out of interior design projects across the commercial and residential sectors, assessed for successful completion, execution, finishes and final detailing.

View the categories
Exclusive Experiences for Winners & Finalists

SBID Awards finalists and winners enjoy numerous benefits from enhanced credibility and increased exposure among the professional design community, to visibility on the website; media and PR opportunities throughout the year, as well as a Finalist/Winner certificate and use of the official Finalist/Winner logos to showcase achievements in your marketing materials and website. Once more for the deserving Global category winners, the coveted SBID International Design Awards crystal trophy presented at the annual Awards ceremony.

Premium Event Experiences

Attendees of the prestigious SBID Awards ceremony include leading, high-profile professionals across the design sectors, trade specialists, and industry media.

Attendance represents a unique opportunity to connect, celebrate and showcase your work to a specialist audience of professional peers, potential business partners, and prospective clients.

*Subject to availability

评审.text:
Skip to main content
Judging
The 3-tier judging process ensures that all entries are carefully scrutinised for technical standards and creative calibre

The Awards attract entries from around the world, representing every continent. The international nature of the competition means judges and voters are able to choose from a wide field, both in terms of types of project, scale, value and creative inspiration. The three-tier voting process ensures that all projects are fairly assessed for their technical merit, creative delivery and ability to inspire by those who appreciate and understand good design.

Step 1

A specially selected Technical Panel of industry specialists carefully assess each entry on its technical merits, eliminating those that do not meet required building and safety standards or the brief. Eligible entries are then assessed by the panel of expert Judges.

Step 2

The Judges are invited to lend their expertise due to their superior standing in the creative fields; they have each achieved a high level of creativity and professionalism in their career.

Step 3

Ultimately, the public are the ultimate consumers and end-users of design. For this reason, a public vote makes up the remaining 10% of scores. Each voter may only cast one vote per category, with IP addresses monitored to ensure fair play.

Technical Panel

LEARN MORE

FAQ.text:
Skip to main content
FAQs
How much does it cost to enter? 

1. Early Bird Rate |  Entry Period: 2 March – 31 March 2026  |  £250

2. Standard Rate  |  Entry Period: 31 March – 29 May 2026  |  £275

3. Extended Rate  |  Entry Period: 29 May – 26 June 2026  |  £325

All entry periods close at 5pm BST. There is a one-off £50 admin fee that is applicable to all entries. This is charged per entrant, not per submission. All prices listed exclude VAT. 

Please note: There are NO deadline extensions. Entries will close on 26 June 2026 at 5pm (BST).

How do I enter the SBID Awards? 

You will need to register to create an account on the SBID Awards entry portal. If you already have an account, you can simply sign in using your credentials. We recommend you download the Entry Guidelines for all you need to know about entering the SBID Awards.

What are the key dates & deadlines?

Subscribe to the SBID Awards newsletter to keep up to date with the latest announcements:

Entries Open: 2 March 2026
Early Bird Entry Closes: 31 March 2026 (5pm BST)
Standard Rate Entry Closes: 29 May 2026 (5pm BST)
Extended Rate Entry Closes: 26 June 2026 (5pm BST)
Category Finalists Announced & Public Vote Opens: 29 July 2026
Public Vote Closes: 4 September 2026 (5pm BST)
Regional Category Winners Announced (online): 7 September 2026
Global Category Winners Announced (live at SBID Awards ceremony): 6 November 2026

Please note: There are NO deadline extensions. Entries will close on 26 June 2026 at 5pm BST.

Are there entry benefits for SBID Members?

SBID Members are offered exclusive entry discounts into the SBID International Design Awards across all Interior Design, Product Design and Fit Out categories. Members receive:

   1 x annual submission free of charge (worth £275).
   Additional entries charged at the reduced Early Bird rate of £250 throughout the full entry period.
   Exclusive 5% discount on SBID Awards ceremony tickets.

There is a one-off £50 admin fee that is applicable to all entries. This is charged per entrant, not per submission. All prices listed exclude VAT. 

For more information about joining the SBID professional community, click here.

What does the entry fee include?

The SBID Awards prides itself on its transparent entry pricing. With no hidden costs to progress in the competition, your entry fee includes the cost of entering your submission, as well as all related publishing opportunities if you are shortlisted. Finalists/Winners receive:

Listing on the SBID Awards website as a Finalist or Winner
Inclusion in the printed annual edition of the SBID Global Interior Design Book
SBID Awards’ Finalist/Winner certificate and use of official logos to showcase achievement
Bespoke crystal trophy for Category Award presented at the SBID International Design Awards ceremony (Winners only!)
Increased opportunities for exposure with SBID, including the chance to feature in related promotions and editorial interviews*

*Editorial publication is not guaranteed to all successful entrants, but may be offered as a benefit to selected Finalists and Winners.

What is the judging process? 

After the entries are reviewed by the Technical Panel, a jury of expert Judges representing a broad range of design skills and expertise across the industry will score the entries. Accounting for 90% of the overall scores, the Judges’ decisions are final and confidential.

The wider public also have the opportunity to vote for their favourite Finalist designs during the online Public Vote which opens when the shortlist is revealed on 29 July and closes on 4 September. The votes will account for the remaining 10% of the overall scores for determining the Global Category Winners. Find out more

How and when are Category Finalists and Winners announced?

Category Finalists will be announced online on 29 July 2026.

Regional Winners will be announced online on 7 September 2026.

Global Category Winners will be announced at the SBID Awards ceremony on 6 November 2026.

All finalists/winners are published on the SBID Awards website. To see last year’s award winners, click here.

What’s the difference between Regional & Global Winners?

All projects submitted into Interior Design categories will be automatically considered for a Global and Regional Award based on project location. Each entry category therefore provides 2 chances to win either as a Regional and/or Global Category Winner.

Regional Category Winners: Highest scoring among the region from the Judges scores. Will be revealed online on the SBID Awards website.

Global Category Winners: Highest scoring across all regions from the Judges scores & the Public Vote combined. Will be announced live at the SBID Awards Ceremony.

All shortlisted Interior Design entries will also be up for a chance to receive the accolade of Overall Winner as the highest scoring entry from the Judges scores & the Public Vote across all regions & entry categories.

If I am a Regional Winner can I still win the Global Category? 

Yes! Being a Regional Winner does not exclude you from winning the Global Category — you are eligible for both! Likewise, even if you are not a Regional Winner, you can still become a Global Winner based on your overall score.

How much does it cost to attend the SBID Awards ceremony?

The cost for individual tickets depends on whether you’re buying a single seat or full tables and in what pricing zone! Explore more info on attending the ceremony, here.

When do SBID Awards ceremony tickets go on sale?

Tickets for the SBID Awards Ceremony 2026 are now on sale! Click here to purchase.

Are there any networking opportunities?

From the Awards’ finalists to the revered panel of international judges, the SBID Awards ceremony represents a unique opportunity to network and celebrate with the industry’s top interior design professionals.

Due to high demand, this year’s ceremony will take place at an even bigger venue! With 500 guests in attendance, the ceremony will provide the perfect setting to immerse yourself within the design community during the Opening Drinks Reception, as well as an After Party to extend the celebrations until late!
